feature: Client-supplied systemCore verbatim injection (the confirmed security hole / key-burn)

phaseRef: Phase 0-A + Part B2 (server owns canonical systemCore per task; client sends structured fields only; auth mandatory, OSMO_REQUIRE_AUTH opt-out deleted)

feature: Shared /api/suggest wire reused by 6 non-drafting LLM call sites (judge, threadIntel, insight, dossier, ask, voicePersona)
residual: none
phaseRef: Part B2 + Phase 0-A (task discriminator + one server-owned canonical core per task; all 7 sites migrate together)

feature: judge (MessageJudge / pill 'Analyze' — score/works/risks/alternatives)
residual: Migration decision is now made — judge is one of the 7 tasks moving to server-owned core + server Safety (0-A 'applies to all 7 tasks'). But the plan never enumerates judge's structured-OUTPUT response schema (score/works/risks/alternatives) nor its specific fallback/error behavior; Phase 1's error taxonomy is explicitly scoped to the interactive drafting path, not the Analyze call.
phaseRef: Phase 0-A / B2

feature: threadIntel (per-thread intel: urgency/action/question/commitments/tone/temp/effort/automated)
residual: Phase 5 now specifies enrichment-cache durability/invalidation (durable, STABLE key, per-device isolation, TTL, reconcile with client 7-day cache), and threadIntel migrates via the task-typed contract. But the plan never decides which intel fields become deterministic FACTS vs LLM JUDGMENT (the field-level 3-tier decomposition is not enumerated), and the cache store's concrete schema is left as 'stable key + TTL' rather than a defined data model.
phaseRef: Phase 5 + Phase 0-A

feature: insight (legacy TOPIC/BRIEF single completion + deterministic-brief fallback)
residual: none
phaseRef: Part B2 + Phase 0-A (insight named among the 6 sites kept and migrated together onto the task-typed contract with a server-owned canonical core)

feature: dossier (person dossier single completion)
residual: Endpoint/contract is covered by the task-typed contract (dossier is one of the 7). But the plan does not specify dossier's entitlement/Pro gating (only general per-account quota in 0-A and entitlement integrity in 0-C) nor any dossier-specific error/fallback behavior. Its plaintext derivative cache is touched only via B3/cross-cutting deletion completeness, not as an endpoint contract.
phaseRef: Phase 0-A / B2

feature: ask (grounded free-form Q&A single completion)
residual: Endpoint/contract (task-typed) and server Safety ('applies to all 7 tasks') are framework-covered, though Safety is specified over goal+intent+transcript, not the free-form ask input. Crucially, per-task quota accounting for ask (that a free-form ask burns a credit) is not specified — the plan defines per-account quota + a global spend circuit-breaker at the aggregate level but never states that an ask decrements a meter.
phaseRef: Phase 0-A

feature: voicePersona (LLM read of the user's own voice)
residual: voicePersona survives as one of the 7 migrating tasks, but the plan does not reconcile the LLM voicePersona read against the Phase-5 retrieval exemplars (Part A only asserts 'retrieval, not fine-tuning' generally), nor specify voicePersona's cache durability or server-side Pro gating. B3 flags it only as a plaintext derivative-cache privacy issue.
phaseRef: Phase 5 / B3

feature: Server-enforced free-tier quota (checkAndConsume, 15 drafts / 7-day epoch bucket, 429)
residual: none
phaseRef: Phase 0-B (quota/trial counters moved to durable Postgres/Supabase) + 0-A (keyed per-account) + Phase 1 (consumed only on success, roll back on failure)

feature: Client-side entitlement metering (Entitlements.decideDraft, 15/week, UX-only)
residual: The server-side half is now solid (durable per-account quota in 0-B/0-A, consume-on-success in Phase 1), but the plan never specifies the client↔server reconciliation contract: how server 'remaining' (x-osmo-drafts-remaining / 429) updates the client meter, or how the two 15-values are kept in lockstep. The named reconciliation in Phase 5 is for the enrichment cache, not the draft meter.
phaseRef: Phase 0-B / 0-A (server side only)

feature: Keyless mock fallback (mockTakes server-side + MockGenerator router fallback)
residual: none
phaseRef: Phase 1 (never silently serve/persist mock as real; label degraded mode; autodraft must not store '[mock]' as sendable) + Phase 0-A (auth mandatory removes keyless bypass; spend circuit-breaker defines the degrade-to-mock path)

feature: Server-side Safety re-run (anti-manipulation guardrail on the server)
residual: The plan relocates Safety to the server (0-A re-runs it on goal+intent+transcript for all 7 tasks; Phase 1 repeats the re-run) but does NOT harden the trivially-bypassable keyword/co-occurrence classifier and does NOT specify the server refusal response shape — exactly the two sub-gaps this row raised remain open.

feature: Client-side Safety pre-check (Safety.check before network)
residual: none

feature: Model selection / hardcoded claude-sonnet-5 + unvalidated model passthrough

But server-side enforcement of the autodraft policy itself — the 30/day cap as a durable server counter and autodraft-specific quota accounting — is not specified; autodraft remains a client-side policy.
phaseRef: Phase 4 + Phase 1 + 0-A/0-B

feature: Background quality gate (S4 generate-2 -> Haiku judge -> S5 conditional repair)
residual: none
phaseRef: Phase 4 (durable job queue + out-of-request worker; resumable, idempotent jobs; budget-account the 2–4x multiplier against 0-A)

residual: none
phaseRef: Part A KEEP (prompt-cache discipline + byte-stable app-bundled core) + B2/0-A (one server-owned canonical core per task with structured fields sent separately, preserving the cached prefix)

feature: Upstream error handling (non-2xx -> 502, no retry/backoff, no streaming)

feature: RECLASSIFICATION of row 2 ('client-supplied systemCore verbatim injection => covered / none')
residual: none

feature: GET /api/sync/pull (cursor pull from oplog)
residual: none
phaseRef: Phase 0-B — names the per-device oplog in durable Postgres (Supabase), a durable gap-free monotonic per-device seq as the cursor source, and retention/compaction; concretely specifies pull's backing store and cursor durability.

feature: POST /api/sync/send (blocking provider send, echo to oplog)
residual: Phase 4 adds a send idempotency key + optimistic echo + server-side send timeout, and 0-B makes the OAuth/provider-token store durable — but there is still no durable send-OUTBOX with retry (the durable job queue in Phase 4 is scoped to the S4/S5 background quality gate, not the send path) and no specified 409/422 provider-send error contract. Double-send is mitigated by the idempotency key but the outbox+retry semantics and the reject/retry contract are unspecified.
phaseRef: Phase 4 (send idempotency key, send timeout) + Phase 0-B (durable OAuth-token store)

feature: GET /api/events (per-device SSE doorbell stream)
residual: 0-B resolves the cross-instance transport (Postgres/Redis pub-sub doorbell fanout OR an enforced single-instance guard), but there is still no reconnect / Last-Event-ID / redelivery contract for the SSE stream, and the ?token= query-string auth surface for /api/events is never addressed (0-A's mandatory device-token auth is scoped to /api/suggest, not the events query-auth channel).
phaseRef: Phase 0-B (SSE across instances OR single-instance guard)

feature: Oplog store — per-device message log (pull substrate)
residual: none
phaseRef: Phase 0-B — specifies the durable per-device oplog table in Postgres, a restart-surviving durable gap-free monotonic seq generator (which entails multi-instance write serialization), and retention/compaction; content lives in Postgres, not the heap.

feature: SSE fanout + publish() doorbell delivery (single-process)
residual: 0-B adds the cross-instance pub/sub bus (Postgres/Redis doorbell fanout) OR an enforced single-instance guard, closing the A-publishes/B-subscribes miss — but the reconcile-fallback guarantee (doorbells are best-effort; a missed doorbell is recovered by the next pull) is never documented, so the safety net behind fanout is left unstated.
phaseRef: Phase 0-B (Postgres/Redis pub-sub doorbell fanout)

feature: Cursor / seq semantics + idempotent re-pull dedup
residual: none
phaseRef: Phase 0-B — explicitly replaces the per-process counter with a 'durable, gap-free, monotonic per-device seq' as the cursor source, restart- and instance-safe; directly cited to coverage sync #6.

feature: Client RealtimeSyncEngine + 401 restart-recovery contract
residual: none
phaseRef: Phase 0-B — explicitly 'Define the device recovery contract so a durable backend does NOT force a 401→cursor-reset→full re-pull on every deploy,' cited to finding client-cursor-reset-on-durable-migration and coverage sync #7.

feature: Doorbell event schema (sync.dirty / connection.status / backfill.progress)
residual: The plan addresses doorbell TRANSPORT (fanout/single-instance) but never mentions the realtime event CONTRACT: the sync.dirty / connection.status / backfill.progress event schema, its versioning, or the 'never send message bodies' invariant on the new transport. grep confirms no schema/versioning/never-send-bodies language anywhere.
phaseRef: none (no phase covers the doorbell event schema or its invariants; 0-B covers only transport)

feature: E2EE multi-device DB sync (SyncEngine + BlobStore) — the whole cross-Mac sync backend
residual: The plan now MENTIONS it (closing 'never mentions') and B3 resolves the privacy contradiction (scope the public claim, add ZDR+DPA, do not advertise E2EE until KDF+key lifecycle real, don't enable static-salt impl). But the actual backend build-out enumerated in the gap — endpoint, per-user blob isolation/auth, conflict semantics, retention — remains unspecified; Phase 6 ('if pursued') only names durable cursors (sync-cursor-not-persisted) plus crypto. The egress is gated off rather than designed.
phaseRef: PART B B3 (scope claim, do not advertise E2EE) + Phase 6 (deferred, if pursued)

feature: Sync encryption: CryptoBox placeholder KDF + static shared salt + key/passphrase management
residual: Phase 6 concretely specifies Argon2id (memory-hard KDF), per-user random salt, and key escrow/recovery/rotation, and forbids enabling the static-salt impl — covering three of the four items. But passphrase/keyphrase PROVENANCE (where the root secret originates / how it is established — user-entered vs device-key-derived vs Keychain) is still unspecified; the KDF inputs' origin is undefined.
phaseRef: Phase 6 (Argon2id + per-user random salt + key escrow/recovery/rotation)

feature: POST /api/connect/link — mint OAuth/hosted-auth/mock URL + create pending link
residual: 0-B durably stores 'pending OAuth links' (durable + multi-instance), but the plan never mentions PKCE at all — so durable storage of the codeVerifier is unspecified — nor single-use/one-time consumption semantics, nor the connect-flow endpoint contract.
phaseRef: Phase 0-B (durable, restart-safe server state)

feature: GET /api/oauth/google/callback — exchange code, store Gmail tokens, seed backfill
residual: Durable encrypted token vault (0-B, 'OAuth tokens encrypted at rest') and refresh scheduling (Phase 4 refresh loop) are covered, but the OAuth callback anti-forgery `state`/CSRF validation is never specified (0-D's CSRF work is only for cookie-authed web POSTs, not the OAuth code-flow callback).
phaseRef: Phase 0-B + Phase 4

feature: GET /api/oauth/slack/callback — exchange code, store Slack user token, seed DM backfill
residual: Durable encrypted Slack-token storage (0-B) and refresh loop (Phase 4, Slack included) are covered, but callback `state`/CSRF hardening is not specified, and invalid-token → re-auth handling is not called out.
phaseRef: Phase 0-B + Phase 4

feature: GET /api/oauth/x/callback — PKCE exchange, store X tokens (~2h expiry), seed DM backfill
residual: none
phaseRef: Phase 0-B (durable encrypted OAuth token store) + Phase 4 (explicit OAuth token refresh loop for X — 'refresh tokens captured then discarded, sends die ~1–2h after connect')

feature: OAuth token storage + refresh lifecycle (cross-cutting: setOAuthTokens/oauthTokens/refreshXToken)
residual: Durable encrypted store (0-B), refresh loop for all providers (Phase 4), and OAuth client-secret rotation (Cross-cutting secrets runbook) are covered, but invalid-token detection → re-auth prompt flow is not specified, and rotation of the token-vault encryption key (as opposed to provider client secrets) is not enumerated.
phaseRef: Phase 0-B + Phase 4 + Cross-cutting (secrets rotation runbook)

feature: POST /api/connect/notify — Unipile hosted-auth callback (bind account_id to pending link)
residual: Signature verification (Cross-cutting 'Webhook signature verification (Unipile)') and durable connection records (0-B) are covered, but idempotent binding of account_id→pending link (dedup on duplicate notify) is not specified.

feature: POST /api/webhooks/unipile — inbound message_received / account.status webhook
residual: Signature verification (Cross-cutting Unipile), durable oplog (0-B), and multi-instance fan-out via Postgres/Redis pub-sub doorbell (0-B) are all covered, but idempotent/dedup ingest of inbound webhooks (Unipile redelivery handling) is not specified, and 0-B offers a 'single-instance guard' as an escape hatch that does not itself provide multi-instance fan-out.
phaseRef: Phase 0-B (durable oplog + pub-sub fanout) + Cross-cutting (webhook signature verification)

residual: none

residual: Signature verification (constructEvent), SDK dependency, routing to durable osmo_subscriptions, and full lifecycle mapping (created/updated/deleted/past_due, cancellation lapses) are all specified in 0-C. But webhook event idempotency/dedupe is never addressed — Stripe can redeliver events, and the only idempotency in the plan (Phase 4) is for outbound message sends, not webhook ingestion.

residual: none

residual: none
phaseRef: Phase 0-C removes the OSMO- prefix mock license and makes durable osmo_subscriptions the real lookup with 7-day offline grace counting down; Phase 2 [ADD] explicitly makes the entitlement signer 'a distinct Ed25519 key domain from the registry signer,' and cross-cutting notes the entitlement-signer↔app-bundle coupling (app trusts only prod pubkey, fails safe to Free).

feature: POST /api/license/reset — dev/keyless-only subscription+trial reset
residual: 0-C establishes a general 'remove or hard-gate no-auth billing bypasses before any paid launch' principle that categorically covers a no-auth reset endpoint, but /api/license/reset is never enumerated and the plan gives no explicit confirmation it stays disabled in live mode (it names only mock-complete and the OSMO- license).

feature: POST /api/trial/start — start server-recorded 14-day trial per device
residual: Abuse prevention on re-register (0-A rate-limit/proof-of-work on /api/device/register, per-account quota) and durable trial counters (0-B) are specified, but the actual trial monetization lifecycle — 14-day start, expiry, and tier-gating/downgrade on expiry — is never designed; trials are treated only as a quota/abuse concern.
phaseRef: Phase 0-A / 0-B

feature: POST /api/promo/redeem — promo / referral codes (trial-extend + discount ack)
residual: The revised plan never mentions promo or referral codes, a promo/referral store, or Stripe coupons anywhere. The hardcoded FRIEND/LAUNCH/WELCOME catalog and coupon-at-checkout application remain entirely uncovered.

feature: Payment-processor integration infrastructure (Stripe SDK, price catalog, customer portal, cancellation/refund/dunning)
residual: 0-C concretely adds the Stripe SDK, plan→price catalog, customer portal (self-serve cancel/manage), cancellation→entitlement (lapse), and dunning (past_due), and cross-cutting adds a secrets-rotation runbook incl. the webhook secret. But the refund→entitlement flow (e.g. charge.refunded revoking Pro) is not specified — the listed lifecycle is created/updated/deleted/past_due only — and explicit Stripe env/secrets wiring in render.yaml is not spelled out.
phaseRef: Phase 0-C + cross-cutting secrets rotation

feature: GET /api/media (binary attachment proxy: device-token auth + per-platform connection-ownership gate)
residual: Phase 0-A makes device-token auth mandatory only on /api/suggest (not /api/media), and 0-B makes connection records durable, but the /api/media endpoint contract itself is never written: no param spec (platform/messageRef/attachmentRef/mime), no explicit 'device must own a connection for this platform' precondition, and no 400/401 semantics for this route.
phaseRef: Phase 0-A (auth) + 0-B (durable connections); cross-cutting line 96

feature: Gmail attachment fetch (Gmail API v1 users/me/messages/*/attachments via stored OAuth access_token)
residual: 0-B makes Google OAuth tokens durable+encrypted-at-rest and Phase 4 adds a Gmail refresh loop, closing the token-source/redeploy-survival half. But the Gmail media-proxy path itself is never described: no 502/404 handling and no fetch contract for users/me/messages/*/attachments.
phaseRef: Phase 0-B (durable OAuth tokens) + Phase 4 (OAuth refresh loop)

feature: Slack attachment fetch (direct url_private fetch + SSRF hostname allowlist files.slack.com)
residual: 0-B makes the Slack token durable and line 96 gestures at generic 'media SSRF hardening', but the Slack-specific pieces are unspecified: no files.slack.com hostname allowlist, no streamed-body passthrough design, and no 400/502/413 error contract.
phaseRef: Phase 0-B (durable Slack token) + cross-cutting line 96 (media SSRF hardening)

feature: Unipile attachment fetch (downloadAttachment for linkedin/whatsapp/instagram; live-key gate, mock→null→placeholder)
residual: Unipile media proxying is entirely unaddressed: no isLiveUnipile() live-vs-mock gating, no 404-on-missing-bytes, no tenant-key handling. The line-96 'media SSRF hardening' does not apply to Unipile's SDK downloadAttachment (no arbitrary URL), and 0-B durable connections only tangentially touch connection-id ownership without writing it for the media path.
phaseRef: none (0-B durable connections only tangential)

feature: No-error placeholder fallback (no connection / no token / mock → 1x1 transparent PNG, cache-control no-store)
residual: The 'always render something' contract for media is never defined. The durable-token redesign in 0-B changes the degradation behavior (tokens now survive restart) but the plan does not state whether/when media still degrades to a 1x1 placeholder vs an error. Phase 1's 'never serve mock as real' applies to drafts, not media bytes.
phaseRef: none

The endpoint is not mentioned.

feature: Web session lifecycle & cookie security (osmo_session httpOnly SameSite=Lax 30-day)
residual: 0-D line 57 closes 'no server-side expiry/rotation' and 'no CSRF', but the durable/multi-instance web-session store is not enumerated in 0-B's durable list (web_sessions absent from line 44), so the per-instance in-memory fallback and cross-instance session validation persist; cookie-signing secret rotation is not called out in the line 92 secrets runbook.

feature: POST /api/account/delete — device-auth purge of in-memory state + cascade delete of user/devices/sub/sessions (data deletion)
residual: Cross-cutting line 94 extends 'deletion completeness across durable stores, server caches, plaintext derivative caches, and the exemplar store' — closing the net-new-state purge obligation — but /api/account/delete idempotency, two-store partial-failure handling, and deletion verification/audit remain unspecified.

feature: /account page — server component, cookie-auth, renders identity + plan/tier + linked Macs, redirects to /login when unauthenticated

The revised plan is backend/API-only.

feature: /login page — email form, calls /api/auth/request, shows dev-mode magic link inline
residual: 0-D line 55 removes verifyUrl from the API response body, which transitively starves the /login page's inline dev-mode link display; but the /login UI and explicit guidance on gating the dev-mode inline-link render are not addressed anywhere.

feature: /download page — 'Coming soon' disabled button, NO actual download/notarized-build link
residual: No mention anywhere in the revised plan of release-artifact distribution, notarized-build hosting, app versioning, or wiring an actual download URL; the /download stub is untouched.

feature: Durable accounts/session data model (Supabase osmo_users/devices/subscriptions/magic_links/web_sessions + in-memory fallback selector)
residual: 0-C covers osmo_subscriptions and 0-B (line 44) covers device tokens durably, but osmo_users, web_sessions, and magic_links tables are not enumerated in the durable-store list, and the MemoryAccountsStore per-instance fallback plus multi-instance session/magic-link validation remain unspecified — the exact durability gap the plan's objective names but does not close for this subsystem.

feature: User data export / portability (GDPR/CCPA access right)
residual: Cross-cutting line 94 now names 'data export/portability (absent everywhere)' as a required ADD (and B3 mentions 'export completeness'), but — unlike deletion, which is scoped store-by-store — export is a one-line commitment with no scope across the specific durable + net-new stores and no format/endpoint/completeness spec.
phaseRef: Cross-cutting GDPR/CCPA (line 94); B3 (line 26)

feature: POST /api/enrich/person endpoint (orchestration contract)
residual: B3 now acknowledges the two paid upstreams (Unipile+Exa) as an egress surface, closing the 'never acknowledges' half. But the endpoint's own contract is still unspecified: no request/response schema, no device-token auth for /api/enrich/person (0-A mandates auth only on /api/suggest), no result-size caps, and no defined placement relative to the drafting pipeline.
phaseRef: Part B B3 (egress acknowledgment); Phase 0-A (auth, but scoped to /api/suggest)

feature: Per-device rate limiting (10 req / 60s sliding window)
residual: Phase 0-B establishes the durable-Postgres pattern and moves quota/trial counters off-heap, and 0-A adds a limiter on /api/device/register — but the enrichment-specific 10/60s sliding-window limiter (the SOLE cost control on Unipile+Exa) is never named as durable/shared state, so it remains a per-process Map that resets on redeploy and diverges across instances. The circuit-breaker in 0-A is Anthropic-only; no cost breaker governs the paid enrichment upstreams.
phaseRef: Phase 0-B (durable counters pattern); Phase 0-A (register limiter, circuit-breaker)

feature: LinkedIn profile fetch via Unipile (getUserProfile)
residual: Connection-state durability is now covered: Phase 0-B moves connection records + OAuth tokens (encrypted at rest) + pending OAuth links to durable Postgres, and cross-cutting adds Unipile webhook signature verification, so LinkedIn is no longer silently wiped on redeploy. Still missing: the degradation contract (behavior when LinkedIn is not connected) and Unipile token refresh/lifecycle — Phase 4's OAuth refresh loop enumerates only Gmail/Slack/X.
phaseRef: Phase 0-B (connection/OAuth durability); Cross-cutting (Unipile webhook sig)

feature: Exa public-web search + retry/backoff
residual: Only B3 acknowledges Exa as a privacy-egress surface. The substantive gap is untouched: no Exa key handling, no retry/backoff policy, no query construction, no result-size caps, and no cost governance. Phase 1's retry taxonomy and 0-A's spend circuit-breaker are both Anthropic-scoped; Phase 2's signed registry remains scoped to LLM directives and does not cover this non-LLM upstream.
phaseRef: Part B B3 (egress mention only); no operational coverage

feature: Keyless deterministic mock enrichment mode
residual: The plan's 'deterministic mock' references (0-A degrade-on-breach, Phase 1 'never serve mock as real') are all drafting-scoped. Keyless mock enrichment is never mentioned, and neither the golden-set eval (Phase 3) nor cold-start/outage handling (Phase 4) is extended to enrichment, so whether mock enrichment persists and how it preserves eval/demo determinism is unaddressed.
phaseRef: none — Phases 1/3/4 are drafting-only

feature: Source-resolution degradation ladder + 502 outage semantics
residual: No enrichment error taxonomy is defined. Phase 1's error taxonomy (Anthropic 429/500/529/timeout, degraded-mode labeling) is drafting-only. The load-bearing distinction between real-outage (502) and legitimate-emptiness (source=none) for the enrichment client is still specified only in code.
phaseRef: none — Phase 1 taxonomy is drafting-only

feature: Client-side PersonEnrichment cache (device-local DB, 7-day staleness, refetch)
residual: Phase 5 now explicitly names the client's 7-day cache and directs the server cache to 'reconcile with' it using a stable key + per-device isolation + TTL — a real improvement over silence. But it states the need without resolving it: which side owns TTL, the key-coherence protocol between client and server, and how double-caching / stale divergence is prevented are still unspecified.
phaseRef: Phase 5 (enrichment cache reconciliation directive)

feature: Consent gating (enrichmentEnabled toggle + remote flag 'enrichment')
residual: B3(c) now extends the privacy story to enrichment egress (name/handle/hints → Unipile+Exa) with scoped claim + ZDR/DPA + erase/export, closing item (c). But (a) the 'enrichment' remote flag is still not specified in the Phase 2 registry (only aiDrafting kill-switch is), and (b) the local enrichmentEnabled consent toggle and its server-side enforcement are never mentioned.
phaseRef: Part B B3 (privacy egress); Phase 2 (registry, but 'enrichment' flag unspecified)

feature: Server-side enrichment cache (Phase 5 proposal)
residual: Phase 5 now specifies a durable backing store (via 0-B Postgres), a STABLE key (not volatile personID), per-device isolation, TTL, and reconciliation with the client's 7 days — most of the prior under-specification is closed. Still unspecified: the exact key composition (name+linkedinHandle+hints?), the invalidation policy, and size caps.
phaseRef: Phase 5 (enrichment cache); Phase 0-B (durable store)

feature: Client offline/retry behavior (keep-last, empty-this-session, force toast)
residual: Enrichment offline/retry semantics are still not addressed. Phase 1's offline/retry work is drafting-scoped, and Phase 5 only mentions cache reconciliation, not runtime failure behavior. The required interaction between the durable server cache and the client's keep-last-on-failure and empty-this-session guards (and force toast) is undefined, so stale/empty coherence remains unspecified.
phaseRef: none — Phase 1 offline/retry is drafting-only; Phase 5 covers cache only

feature: GET /api/config/flags — remote feature flags + kill-switch (aiDrafting/autodraft/enrichment/media/webhooks)
residual: none
phaseRef: Phase 2 ([FIX] server-side kill-switch enforcement so /api/suggest actually checks aiDrafting; [ADD] fetch→verify→cache→offline-fallback path defining which flag values apply on signature failure) + Phase 0-A key protection

feature: GET /api/health — uptime probe + incident-banner broadcast (OSMO_STATUS / OSMO_STATUS_MESSAGE)
residual: No health-probe endpoint or incident-banner (OSMO_STATUS) mechanism is defined; the registry is scoped to 'volatile config only' and is not extended to carry a status banner; per-instance liveness/readiness probes are unspecified. Only tangential gestures exist (Observability crash-alerting is operator-facing; Deploy graceful-drain implies but does not spec readiness).
phaseRef: Not addressed; nearest tangential mentions are Cross-cutting Observability (crash alerting) and Cross-cutting Deploy/rollout (graceful drain), neither of which defines a health endpoint or incident banner

feature: GET /api/version — update-check stub (hardcoded 0.2.0 / build 2 / downloadURL / notes)
residual: Version/update-check delivery is still unowned. Phase 2 explicitly keeps the registry scoped to volatile config only, and no mechanism is specified to publish version/build/downloadURL/notes without editing and redeploying the hardcoded payload — contradicting the 'iterate without OTA' goal.

residual: The feedback/bug-report store is not in the enumerated 0-B durability list (oplog, tokens, OAuth, quota/trial, connections, pending links); no durable storage or routing is specified, so it stays ephemeral. Privacy reconciliation for free-text feedback that may contain message content is not addressed by the B3 privacy-claim rescoping.
phaseRef: Not addressed; Phase 0-B durability list omits feedback and B3/GDPR cross-cutting items do not cover free-text feedback

feature: POST /api/dev/emit — E2E trigger to inject a scripted inbound message (mock-mode only, device-auth)
residual: Phase 0 removes the mock-complete Pro-grant and OSMO- license payment bypasses (0-C) but never env-gates or removes /api/dev/emit. Mock-mode remains the only gate, so a keyless prod deploy (no UNIPILE_DSN/API_KEY ⇒ mock mode) still exposes inbound-message injection to any registered device.

feature: GET /api/dev/outbox — E2E assertion surface returning recorded /api/sync/send outbox (mock-mode only, device-auth)
residual: Same as /api/dev/emit — the plan never env-gates or removes /api/dev/outbox for prod. A keyless prod deploy still exposes another device's-scoped outbox as an information surface to any registered device; the security workstreams (0-A/0-C) do not touch dev routes.
phaseRef: Not addressed; Phase 0 security workstreams (0-A/0-C) do not env-gate or remove dev routes

feature: instrumentation.ts register() — boot-time Unipile webhook registration (once per server process)
residual: Boot-time Unipile webhook registration under multi-instance is not specifically addressed. It is only incidentally resolved IF the 0-B/Deploy 'enforced single-instance guard' path is chosen; under the plan's offered alternative (Postgres/Redis pub-sub SSE across instances), N instances would each still reconcile the same tenant-wide hooks via the per-process latch. Cross-cutting webhook signature verification covers inbound verification, not registration reconciliation.
phaseRef: Phase 0-B (SSE-across-instances OR enforced single-instance guard) + Cross-cutting Deploy/rollout (enforced instance-count guard)

The Secrets runbook only ambiguously references 'both/all Ed25519 signers' (the two named domains are entitlement + registry), and security-critical psychology-core changes still require an OTA the plan does not cover.

residual: Only the two highest-risk unauth endpoints are throttled (0-A rate-limit/PoW on /api/device/register; 0-D rate-limit on /api/auth/request). There is no cross-cutting shared throttling substrate across ALL endpoints, general per-endpoint DoS on other routes is unmitigated, and the enrich limiter's defeat-by-restart/second-instance is not specifically addressed (0-B moves quota counters to Postgres but does not name the enrich limiter or a shared rate-limit store).

residual: none
phaseRef: Phase 0-A ([ADD] Global Anthropic spend circuit-breaker: rolling daily+monthly budget; on breach → degrade to deterministic mock + page operator) + per-account quota, Pro metering, and register rate-limit closing the trial-abuse/keyless-bypass vectors

feature: Deploy/rollout strategy: state-loss on every redeploy, no graceful drain / migrations / instance-count guard
residual: none
phaseRef: Cross-cutting Deploy/rollout ([ADD] migration ordering for new durable stores; graceful drain; enforced instance-count guard) + Phase 0-B durable Postgres state eliminating wipe-on-redeploy

feature: Secrets rotation & key-compromise runbook (Anthropic key, OAuth client secrets, entitlement signer, webhook secret)
residual: none
phaseRef: Cross-cutting Secrets rotation runbook ([ADD] Anthropic key, OAuth client secrets, webhook secret, all Ed25519 signers; notes entitlement-signer ↔ app-bundle coupling requiring rotation coordinated with an app release)

feature: Identity-graph rebuild on sync + merge-decision durability / cross-device coherence
residual: The plan now has a dedicated CROSS-CUTTING item (line 95) that names 'stable Person IDs (not min-contact-UUID)' and 'synced merge decisions,' gates them 'before any multi-device sync,' and cites the exact findings (person-id-split-brain, rejected-merge-resurrect, C3) — a genuine advance from 'absent.' But it is a one-line goal statement, not a mechanism. Three real sub-gaps remain: (1) STORAGE/DURABILITY is unwired — Phase 0-B's durable-Postgres enumeration (oplog, device tokens, OAuth tokens, quota/trial counters, connections, pending links) omits the identity graph and the merge-decision sets (rejectedPairKeys/confirmed merges), so where these are persisted and how they survive redeploy is unspecified; (2) RECONCILIATION SEMANTICS are undefined — 'synced merge decisions' states the intent but not the conflict-resolution rule (e.g., monotonic union of rejected pairs, confirmed-merge precedence) two Macs need to converge; (3) the REBUILD-ON-SYNC FLOW itself — how the on-device union-find resolver re-clustering consumes synced decisions to avoid resurrecting a rejected pair after DB sync — is not described.
phaseRef: Part C CROSS-CUTTING 'Identity coherence for multi-device' (line 95); depends on Phase 0-B durable state (lines 43-45) and Phase 6 E2EE sync gate (line 87)

feature: Anthropic data-retention / ZDR / DPA dependency behind the 'keep none, never train' privacy claim
residual: B3 now explicitly names the Anthropic upstream retention dependency (default 30-day) and prescribes adding ZDR + DPA and scoping the public claim precisely — closing most of the gap. But two real sub-gaps remain: (1) sub-processor disclosure, explicitly listed in the original gap, is not mentioned anywhere in the plan; (2) the ZDR+DPA fix lives only as a Part B narrative directive and is never operationalized into a Part C workstream with an owner, sequencing, or a launch gate — unlike the money/account items in 0-A/0-C/0-D. Since the public 'keep none/never train' claim rides on the ZDR agreement actually being executed, signing ZDR/DPA before that claim ships should be a hard launch blocker, and the plan does not make it one. Phase 3's log-scrub CI test only proves OSMO's own logs and cannot verify Anthropic upstream retention, so it does not cover this dependency.
phaseRef: Part B §B3 (retention/ZDR/DPA/claim-scoping correction); supported by Phase 3 log-scrub and cross-cutting GDPR/CCPA
